fieldsDetail.name: GEM Mine ID
fieldsDetail.definition: A unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each coal mine by GEM for internal identification purposes.

fieldsDetail.name: MSHA ID
fieldsDetail.definition: A unique numerical identifier assigned to each US coal mine by the US Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) for identification purposes nationwide.

fieldsDetail.name: Country / Area
fieldsDetail.definition: The geographic country or area where a coal mine or proposed coal mine project is located.

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Name
fieldsDetail.definition: The name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.
fieldsDetail.other_rules: Must be unique unless the Mine ID is the same, then we consider it a unit within the mine project parent, duplicates can occur because official Mine names are duplicated within the industry

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Name AKAs
fieldsDetail.null_is_what: Not found, Not applicable, Not yet researched
fieldsDetail.definition: The nickname or alias of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project. AKAs are often used with proposed projects which have not yet been assigned an official name.

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Name (Non-ENG)
fieldsDetail.definition: The non-english name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project, as it appears in the official language of the country or area where the mine is located.

fieldsDetail.name: Complex Name
fieldsDetail.definition: The name of the larger mining complex or group to which one or more coal mines belong.

fieldsDetail.name: GEM Wiki Page (ENG)
fieldsDetail.definition: The URL corresponding to a coal mine's or proposed coal mine project's English GEM wiki page.

fieldsDetail.name: GEM Wiki Page (Non-ENG)
fieldsDetail.definition: The URL corresponding to a coal mine's or proposed coal mine project's non-English GEM wiki page.

fieldsDetail.name: Status
fieldsDetail.definition: "Status" describes the various active/inactive stages of a coal mine or mine project.

values_definitions.Operating: A coal mine where coal removal is occurring for commercial purposes. This includes mines that have produced coal within the year, even if operations were temporarily suspended for several months.
values_definitions.Proposed: A coal mine project that has not yet begun operation. This includes projects that have been announced (via corporate or government planning documents), projects that are under permitting (including feasibility studies), projects that are under construction, as well as those in test operation.
values_definitions.Mothballed: A coal mine that is neither operating nor retired, such as those that are idled or put on "care and maintenance." Such mines could return to operation, but are not currently operating.
values_definitions.Cancelled: A proposed coal mine project for which a cancellation announcement has been made by the owning company or government authorities. GEM also assigns a status of "cancelled" to projects when no progress has been observed for 4+ years.
values_definitions.Shelved: A proposed coal mine project for which no public updates nor progress has been observed for 2+ years.
values_definitions.Retired: A previously operating coal mine that has been permanently closed.

fieldsDetail.name: Status Detail
fieldsDetail.definition: "Status detail" describes the mine status on a granular level, such as which planning stage a proposed coal mine project has progressed to, or whether an operating mine is experiencing temporary work stoppages.

values_definitions.Announced: A status assigned when a coal mine project has been described in corporate or government plans, but full details remain unknown.
values_definitions.Exploration: A status assigned when the economic viability of a coal mine project is being explored. During the exploration stage, information is gathered about a potential mine site's characteristics via geological surveys, pre-feasability studies, and the drilling of boreholes.
values_definitions.Pre-Permit: A status assigned when permit applications (environmental assessments, land use rights, water licenses, etc.) for a coal mine project have been filed, but not yet obtained.
values_definitions.Permitted: A status assigned to a coal mine project that has been granted permit approvals, but has not yet begun construction.
values_definitions.Construction: A status assigned when a coal mine project has begun construction of its mining infrastructure and facilities.
values_definitions.Test Operation: A status assigned when a coal mine project begins coal removal, but not yet for commercial purposes.
values_definitions.Care and Maintenance: A status assigned when an operating mine temporarily closes, but there is potential to recommence operations at a later date. During the care and maintenance period, coal production stops but the mine site is still managed by minimal staff to ensure it remains in a safe and stable condition.

fieldsDetail.name: Project Type
fieldsDetail.definition: Refers to the type of coal mine project: expansion, extension, or new.

values_definitions.New: A "new" mine is a greenfield mine planned to be developed in an area where there was no previous mining activity.
values_definitions.Expansion: An "expansion" is when an existing mine seeks to increase its existing coal capacity.
values_definitions.Extension: An "extension" is when an existing mine seeks to mine new areas to extend its mine life.

fieldsDetail.name: Project Phase
fieldsDetail.definition: Refers to proposed coal mine projects that will be developed in stages, often with initial development followed by subsequent expansions over time (e.g., Phase I, II, III).

fieldsDetail.name: Owners
fieldsDetail.definition: The immediate owner of a coal mine or coal mine project who is responsible for the mining of the coal. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

fieldsDetail.name: Owners GEM Entity ID
fieldsDetail.definition: Unique identifier assigned by the GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker team. Renamed from 'Owner GEM Entity ID' in the May-2026 release; format also changed from 'E12345 [100%]' to 'E12345' (ownership percentages are now exclusively in the Global Energy Ownership Tracker, not denormalized into this cell).

fieldsDetail.name: Owners (Non-ENG)
fieldsDetail.definition: The non-english name of the immediate owner of the coal mine or proposed coal mine project, as it appears in the official language of the country or area where the mine is located.

fieldsDetail.name: Parent Company
fieldsDetail.definition: The highest-level owner of a coal mine or coal mine project. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

fieldsDetail.name: Company HQs
fieldsDetail.definition: The geographic country or area where the parent company is headquartered in.

fieldsDetail.name: Capacity (Mtpa)
fieldsDetail.definition: The maximum amount of coal a mine or mine project is engineered to produce, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

fieldsDetail.name: Production (Mtpa)
fieldsDetail.definition: The amount of coal produced by a mine, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

fieldsDetail.name: Year of Production
fieldsDetail.definition: The year during which the cited coal production occurred. (If coal output for the current year is unavailable, data for the most recently available year is given.)

fieldsDetail.name: Coal Output (Annual, Mst)
fieldsDetail.definition: The amount of coal produced by a US mine, expressed in millions of short tons per year. This value applies to US coal mines only.

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Type
fieldsDetail.definition: The classification of coal mines by mining method, including "Surface," "Underground," and combined "Underground & Surface" facilities.
fieldsDetail.other_notes: should potentially convert from string to array, since there are two types, but mines can be either or both

fieldsDetail.name: Mining Method
fieldsDetail.definition: The specific technique used to extract coal from the earth.

values_definitions.Open Pit: also known as “Open Cast”, refers to mining directly on the ground surface
values_definitions.Strip: is a type of surface mining where the overlying vegetation, rock, and soil (overburden) is removed in order to reach the underlying coal seam, which usually lies a few feet beneath the surface.
values_definitions.Mountain Top Removal: is a mining practice where the tops of mountains are removed, exposing the seams of coal. It is a form of surface mining at the summit or summit ridge of a mountain.
values_definitions.Board and Pillar: also known as “Room and Pillar”, it’s a method of underground mining. In this method, seams of coal are mined partially, leaving large pillars of coal intact to support the overlying layers of rock
values_definitions.Longwall: is a type of underground mining method used to extract coal from a seam that is too deep for open-pit mining

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Size (Km2)
fieldsDetail.definition: The total surface area of the mining covered by the coal mining operation, including surface facilities, access roads, overburden dumps, and the underground or open-pit mine footprint.

fieldsDetail.name: Mine Depth (m)
fieldsDetail.definition: The vertical distance from the ground surface down to the coal seam that is being mined.

fieldsDetail.name: Depth Accuracy
fieldsDetail.definition: Indicates the confidence level in the reported depth of the coal mine. "Exact" means the depth is directly stated in a reliable source, while "Estimated" means it is inferred from averages at the national level in a variety of industry and government reports.

fieldsDetail.name: Workforce Size
fieldsDetail.definition: The total number of personnel employed by a coal mining operation, including both direct employees and contractors working on-site.

fieldsDetail.name: Workforce Accuracy
fieldsDetail.definition: Indicates the accuracy level of workforce data. "Exact" means the workforce size is explicitly reported in data sources, while "Estimated" refers to figures generated using GEM’s machine learning model when official data is unavailable.

fieldsDetail.name: Coal Type
fieldsDetail.definition: The classification of coal based on its physical and chemical properties, particularly its carbon content, calorific value, moisture, volatile matter, and ash content. Global Coal Mine Tracker use British coal type (lignite, subbituminous, bituminous, anthracite).

fieldsDetail.name: Coal Grade
fieldsDetail.definition: The classification of coal based on its primary use: -Thermal Coal: Used for power generation or industrial heating. -Metallurgical Coal (Coking Coal): Used for steelmaking and other industrial processes.

fieldsDetail.name: Percentage of Met Coal
fieldsDetail.definition: The proportion of metallurgical coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for opearting coal mines.

fieldsDetail.name: Percentage of Thermal Coal
fieldsDetail.definition: The proportion of thermal coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for operating coal mines.

fieldsDetail.name: Accuracy Rating
fieldsDetail.definition: Indicates the confidence level in the accuracy of the information provided. A percentage split is marked as "Exact" when it is explicitly stated in the data source, and "Estimated" when it is based on the researcher’s interpretation or experience drawn from available sources.

fieldsDetail.name: Reported Year of Percentage Split
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the percentage breakdown of coal grade was reported or deocumented.

fieldsDetail.name: Total Reserves (Proven and Probable, Mt)
fieldsDetail.definition: The combined amount of economically recoverable or minable coal.

fieldsDetail.name: Year of Total Reserves Recorded
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the total coal reserves of the mine were reported or evaluated.

fieldsDetail.name: Total Resource (Inferred, Indicated, Measured)
fieldsDetail.definition: The total estimated volume of a coal deposit in the ground

fieldsDetail.name: Reserve to Production Ratio (R/P)
fieldsDetail.definition: A measure of how long a coal reserve will last at the current annual rate of production.

fieldsDetail.name: Opening Year
fieldsDetail.definition: The year when the mine commenced its commercial operations.

fieldsDetail.name: Closing Year
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the coal mine is scheduled to close or has already ceased operations.

fieldsDetail.name: Reported Life of Mine
fieldsDetail.definition: The expected operational lifespan of a mine, expressed in year, based on the currently known and economically recoverable reserves, and the planned annual production rate.

fieldsDetail.name: Reported Year of Mine Life
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the life of mine is reported or evaluated.

fieldsDetail.name: Coalfield
fieldsDetail.definition: Coalfield to which a coal mine belongs.

fieldsDetail.name: Latitude
fieldsDetail.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

fieldsDetail.name: Longitude
fieldsDetail.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

fieldsDetail.name: Location Accuracy
fieldsDetail.definition: Indicates whether the location is "Approximate" or "Exact". Location is "Approximate" based on using nearest city or inferred from contextual information in the data source. Location is "Exact" when explicitly provided in the source materials.

fieldsDetail.name: Primary Consumer, Destination
fieldsDetail.definition: The main end user or receiving entity of the coal produced by the mine. This may include coal power plants, steel plants, export terminals, or industrial facilities that rely on coal as a primary source.

fieldsDetail.name: Primary Consumer, Destination Name
fieldsDetail.definition: The name of the end user or receiving entity of the coal. The project name should match exactly with those documented by GEM to ensure consistency. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal' in the May-2026 release; same field, broader scope (any primary consumer/destination, not just plants/terminals).

fieldsDetail.name: Primary Consumer, Destination GEM Wiki
fieldsDetail.definition: GEM's Wiki URLs of end user or receiving entity of the coal. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal GEM Wiki' in the May-2026 release.

fieldsDetail.name: Gas-level Rating
fieldsDetail.definition: The gas-level rating of a coal mine is a classification system that indicates the methane content and gas-related risk level of a coal mine.

fieldsDetail.name: Gas-level Rating Appraisal Year
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the gas-level rating of the coal mine was appraised or officially reported.

fieldsDetail.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
fieldsDetail.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes per year.

fieldsDetail.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)
fieldsDetail.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) per year.
fieldsDetail.unit_name_long: thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent per year

fieldsDetail.name: Year of Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions
fieldsDetail.definition: The year in which the coal mine methane emissions were reported or released.
associated_field: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)

fieldsDetail.name: Source ID
fieldsDetail.definition: The identifier assigned by the reporting agency to the coal mine associated with the reported methane emissions.

fieldsDetail.name: Has associated plume data
fieldsDetail.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a remotely sensed methane plume detailed in the Global Methane Emitters Tracker. Yes* refers to the one mine (Mandalong Coal Mine) which had methane plumes associated with it in the December 2024 briefing which were not included in the version of GMET public at the time due to minor differences in query methodology.

fieldsDetail.name: Has associated CMM mitigation data
fieldsDetail.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a known coal mine methane mitigation project (e.g., pre-mining drainage, flaring, ventilation air methane destruction, utilization, etc.).

fieldsDetail.name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (MCM/yr)
fieldsDetail.definition: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine calculated by GEM, expressed in million cubic meters per year (MCM/yr). This estimate is based on the Model for Calculating Coal Mine Methane (MC2M).

fieldsDetail.name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (M tonnes/yr)
fieldsDetail.definition: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine, expressed in million metric tonnes per year.

fieldsDetail.name: CMM Emissions (CO2e 20 years)
fieldsDetail.definition: An estimate of coal mine methane emissions, expressed in carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) over a 20-year time horizon.
fieldsDetail.unit_name_long: carbon dioxide equivalents over a 20-year time horizon.

fieldsDetail.name: CMM Emissions (CO2e 100 years)
fieldsDetail.definition: CMM Emissions (CO2e 100 years)
fieldsDetail.unit_name_long: carbon dioxide equivalents over a 20-year time horizon.

fieldsDetail.name: Parent GEM Entity ID
fieldsDetail.definition: A static unique identifier assigned by GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker (GEOT) to the highest-level parent owner of a coal mine.

fieldsDetail.name: Location (Non-ENG)
fieldsDetail.definition: The non-English name of the closest city/town to a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.

fieldsDetail.name: Reference Type
fieldsDetail.definition: Indicates the official source (national government data or company report) where the reported coal mine methane figures were found.

fieldsDetail.name: Methane Emissions Factor
fieldsDetail.definition: Methane Emissions Factor

fieldsDetail.name: Methane Gas Content (m^3/tonne)
fieldsDetail.definition: Methane Gas Content (m^3/tonne)

fieldsDetail.name: Mine boundary and methane emissions sources data available?
fieldsDetail.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that a coal mine boundary/boundaries and potential sources of coal mine methane have been identified for this GCMT asset.

fieldsDetail.name: Has mine boundary and methane emissions sources data?
fieldsDetail.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that a coal mine boundary/boundaries and potential sources of coal mine methane have been identified for this GCMT asset.
